2604.14856.title: ClimateCause: Complex and Implicit Causal Structures in Climate Reports
2604.14856.paper_summary: Understanding climate change requires reasoning over complex causal networks. Yet, existing causal discovery datasets predominantly capture explicit, direct causal relations. We introduce ClimateCause, a manually expert-annotated dataset of higher-order causal structures from science-for-policy climate reports, including implicit and nested causality. Cause-effect expressions are normalized and disentangled into individual causal relations to facilitate graph construction, with unique annotations for cause-effect correlation, relation type, and spatiotemporal context. We further demonstrate ClimateCause's value for quantifying readability based on the semantic complexity of causal graphs underlying a statement. Finally, large language model benchmarking on correlation inference and causal chain reasoning highlights the latter as a key challenge.
2604.14856.paper_summary_zh: 理解氣候變化需要對複雜的因果網絡進行推理。<lb/>然而，現有的因果發現數據集主要捕捉明確的、直接的因果關係。<lb/>我們介紹了ClimateCause，一個由專家手動標註的數據集，包含來自科學政策氣候報告的高階因果結構，包括隱含和嵌套的因果性。<lb/>因果表達式被標準化並解構為單獨的因果關係，以促進圖形構建，並提供獨特的標註以表示因果相關性、關係類型和時空背景。<lb/>我們進一步展示了ClimateCause在基於因果圖的語義複雜性量化可讀性方面的價值。<lb/>最後，對相關推斷和因果鏈推理的大型語言模型基準測試突顯了後者作為一個關鍵挑戰。
2604.14856.author: Liesbeth Allein et.al.
2604.14856.authors: Liesbeth Allein, Nataly Pineda-Castañeda, Andrea Rocci, Marie-Francine Moens
